column4: What it means
The enemy with the least Health.
The highest-Health enemy within 2 hexes (Jax's leap).
The N lowest-Health enemies by star level (Karthus). Count splits one instance each.
The ally with the least Health.
The ally with the most Health (Neeko cosplays them).
The lowest-Health allies (Shen shields the 2 lowest).
The nearest enemy.
The farthest enemy on the board.
The 3 nearest enemies (Lulu's 3rd-cast stun hits each).
The 4 farthest enemies — one shot each (Caitlyn).
The Count nearest enemies, one instance each — no enemy is targeted twice (Ziggs' bomblets). Unlike 'Nearest N enemies' the set SIZE follows Count, so it survives a Count that grows per cast.
The farthest enemy NOT already carrying this skill's Mark — so repeat casts spread the mark outward instead of re-marking one unit (Akali's shuriken).
The closest ally (Lillia's secondary heal).

column2: Closest enemy behind Step 1 Aim target
column4: The enemy directly behind an earlier step's target (a pierce's second victim).
The nearest enemy to a SUMMON's owner (the summon's own attack target).
The champion's current attack target — the default aim.
A freshly re-acquired current target after a kill (Darius' recast).
The current target plus the 3 nearest enemies (Twisted Fate's cards split among them).
The current target and the enemy on each side of it (Karma's 3rd cast).
A random enemy within 2 hexes of the current target, re-rolled per instance (Jinx).
The enemy that was knocked up.
Every enemy carrying the Mark a previous step applied (Quinn's arrow rain).
The enemy that just entered attack range (an On-Enemy-Enters-Range trigger's target).
The 3 enemies that dealt the most damage to the caster during a preceding window — it is resolved from damage taken, not from position (Pantheon).

Whatever an earlier Step 1 aimed at (a sequenced follow-up / delayed blast rides it).

column4: The hex where an earlier step's projectile actually landed. An AOE that detonates on impact centres HERE — not on the unit the projectile was aimed at, which is a different claim: a First-Hit projectile can stop on whoever it meets first, and re-reading 'Current'/'Clustered' at detonation time can drift to a hex the projectile never reached.
column5: or the centre of a self-aimed AOE.

column2: Aim Target is now a VALIDATED vocabulary (2026-07-18, the user's call): it names WHICH unit(s) an action targets, and every Hero cell must be a key here. It absorbed the target-SELECTION values that used to live in 'Spread' (Same target folded into the plain single-target aim; Each to its own target, Split across nearest/farthest N, Split across marked, Current + Left + Right became the 'enemy set' / 'per-instance' rows here). 'Spread' kept only geometric SHAPE and was renamed 'Volley Shape'.

'Kind' groups the values (single enemy / enemy set / single ally / ally set / self / position / per-instance / reference) — it is documentation, not a separate checked column. A multi-instance action at an 'enemy set' aim SPLITS its Count among that set; at a single aim it converges. '—' means no aim (a passive self-buff with Recipient Self, an untethered summon).
